data.title: Is the Roaring Kitty effect over?
data.description:
After a cryptic post from Keith Gill, known online as Roaring Kitty, shares of GameStop (NYSE:GME) and AMC Entertainment (NYSE:AMC) popped on Thursday.

Shares of the videogame retailer jumped as much as 14%, but paired most of their gains to end the day up at 5.9%. The theater chain also paired some of its gains, closing higher at 5.9%.

Gill gained popularity during the January 2021 meme stock craze after igniting a frenzy of activity in GameStop (GME) shares. Known for a cult following among some retail investors, he uses social media platforms like YouTube and Reddit to disclose his trading positions.

However, GameStop (GME) ended flat for the week and AMC (AMC) ended down. And this came at a time that Goldman says should be the height of retail trading, with people on vacation, with a spike in way-out-of-the-money calls lately.

"I don’t think Roaring Kitty has any edge in GameStop, and his last big proclamation was 'I believe in management,'" said Wedbush analyst Michael Pachter. "They’ve done nothing since, and the enthusiasm is definitely more muted than the last time."

GameStop (GME) is scheduled to release its third quarter earnings report next week, with loss per share of $0.03 on revenues of $887.68 million.

GameStop (GME) shares have risen nearly 66% so far this year, beating the S&P 500's gain of about 28%.

data.title: Trump team contacts Google, Meta, Snap over online drug sales: report
data.description:
President-elect Donald Trump's transition team has invited five major tech companies, including Meta (NASDAQ:META), Google (NASDAQ:GOOG) (NASDAQ:GOOGL) and Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT), to a meeting in mid-December about dealing with the sale of drugs online, The Information reported Sunday, citing a person with direct knowledge of the correspondence.

According to the report, a representative for James Carroll, who served as director of the Office of National Drug Control Policy during Trump's first term, emailed staffers from the three tech giants, and those from Snap (NYSE:SNAP) and TikTok, to invite them to a call.

The Trump transition team did not immediately respond to a Seeking Alpha request for comment.

Meta (META), Google (GOOGL) (GOOG), Microsoft (MSFT), Snapchat (SNAP) and TikTok did not immediately respond to a Seeking Alpha request for comment.

The transition officials want to hear from the companies about their priorities and any challenges to addressing the issues around the sales of drugs online, the report added.

Trump has promised to crack down on fentanyl coming into the U.S., threatening a 25% tariff on imports from China and Mexico until the countries crack down on illegal immigration and drug smuggling.

In 2023, the FBI and Justice Department investigated Snapchat’s role in the spread and sale of fentanyl-laced pills in the U.S. In March, U.S. prosecutors in Virginia reportedly investigated whether Meta (META) profited from the sale of drugs on Facebook and Instagram.

In January, eBay (EBAY) agreed to pay $59 million and enhance its compliance program to resolve allegations that it violated the Controlled Substances Act in connection with thousands of pill presses and encapsulating machines that were sold through its website.

data.title: ANZ Banking Group names Nuno Matos as CEO
Payment processing firms track robust spending on Black Friday-Cyber Monday
data.description:
With Black Friday weekend now in the rearview mirror, payment technology companies are releasing their insights from the traditional start of the holiday shopping season. Here's what payment companies are highlighting:

  • Fiserv (NYSE:FI): The company's SpendTrend data from Nov. 27-Dec. 2 showed 5.7% Y/Y growth in same-store retail sales, reflecting a 6.7% increase in transactions, while average ticket size was roughly flat at -0.9%. The data tracks card-based payments.

Retail sales saw the most growth on Saturday, with total sales rising 3.0% on Black Friday, 6.7% on the Saturday after Thanksgiving, and 4.0% on Cyber Monday. Purchases in physical stores made up 58% of all retail sales, with sales up 4.6% and transactions increasing 3.0%.

Credit accounted for 64% of card spending at retail, compared with 35% for debt. However, debit spending growth of 8.9% outpaced credit's 4.1% increase in spending.

Small business retail sales jumped 11.9% for Thanksgiving Week 2024, due to a 9.3% increase in transactions and a 2.8% boost in average ticket size, according to Fiserv Small Business Index data. Retail sales gathered speed over the weekend, with 8.6% growth on Black Friday, +9.0% on the Saturday after Thanksgiving, and a 13.0% increase on Cyber Monday.

General merchandise clocked in the highest growth in sales (+20.8%) and transactions (+18.7%).

Furniture, furnishings, electronics, and appliances' sales grew 7.9%, but transactions fell by 2.3%.

Clothing store sales gained 8.6%, coupled with a 2.0% drop in transactions.

Building materials/DIY sales rose 13.6% and transactions climbed 10.8%.

Sporting goods, hobby, etc, saw total retail sales rise 9.2%, along with an 11.8% boost in transactions.

  • Mastercard (NYSE:MA): U.S. retail sales, excluding automotive, rose 3.4% on Black Friday, Nov. 29, from a year ago, with online sales growth of 14.6% Y/Y, outpacing the in-store sales fain of 0.7%.

Steve Sadove, a former Saks Fifth Avenue CEO and current senior adviser to Mastercard said shoppers are "more strategic in their shopping though, prioritizing promotions that they believe hold the greatest value — opening their wallets, but with more intentional distribution."

Jewelry, electronics, and apparel were top gift sectors, with apparel showing particular strength in e-commerce on Black Friday. Grocery spending grew as consumers stocked up for holiday feasting in the two weeks leading up to Black Friday (er, Thanksgiving), while restaurant spending was especially strong on Black Friday.

By region, Massachusetts; Washington, DC; and Colorado outspent other parts of the country.

The Mastercard SpendingPulse data represents all payment types in select markets around the world.

  • Block (NYSE:SQ): The parent of Square, Afterpay and Cash App recorded 144M consumer transactions across its platforms on over the Black Friday-Cyber Monday weekend, a 17% Y/Y increase, and a record for the company. Saturday, Nov. 30, was the most popular shopping day of the weekend.

Online cart sizes were twice as large as in-person shopping carts. Sales of gift card logged in at 450K, up 29% Y/Y.

Buy Now, Pay Later transactions through Block's (NYSE:SQ) Afterpay jumped 10% Y/Y, with Millennials driving usage at 44%, followed by Gen Z at 26%. Beyond fashion and beauty, the largest Afterpay item categories were personal care, household supplies, health care, gift cards, and office supplies.

  • PayPal Holdings (NASDAQ:PYPL): The company logged its biggest day of the year on Cyber Monday, with ~$7B in payments processed at ~$81K per second. More than 1.2M small- to medium-sized businesses had their best sales day of 2024 during the weekend. The company reported 70M+ of SMB transactions on PayPal on Cyber Monday.

Buy Now, Pay Later transactions saw double-digit growth compared with Cyber 5 last year. Pay with Venmo transactions also rose by double digits, PayPal CEO Alex Chriss wrote in a post.

Ranking payment tech stocks, the SA Quant system awarded Strong Buy ratings to Dave (DAVE), PayPal (NASDAQ:PYPL), BILL (BILL), Lightspeed (LSPD), and Block (SQ).

data.title: Stocks with best odds of risk-adjusted returns deserve attention: Goldman Sachs
data.description:
Investors should focus on large-cap stocks with the highest prospective risk-adjusted returns, according to strategists at financial-services firm Goldman Sachs. They revised their basket of stocks with a high Sharpe ratio – a comparison of how well a stock performs in relation to a risk-free investment such as U.S. Treasuries – to include 39 new stocks.

“The median stock in the rebalanced basket has returned -2% year-to-date, compared with a 19% gain for the typical stock in the S&P 500 (SP500,” David Kostin, strategist at Goldman Sachs, said in a December 6 report. “However, looking forward, the median constituent is expected to generate nearly four times the return of the median S&P 500 stock during the next 12 months.”

data.title: Former Intel CEO asks people to pray and fast for Intel employees
data.description:
Ousted Intel (NASDAQ:INTC) chief executive Pat Gelsinger asked people on Saturday to pray and fast for the chipmaker's 100,000 employees as investors wonder about the future of the semiconductor giant.

"This week I'd invite you to join me in praying and fasting for the 100K Intel employees as they navigate this difficult period," Gelsinger wrote in a post on X. "Intel and its team is of seminal importance to the future of the industry and US."

Gelsinger stepped down in a surprise announcement earlier in the week, replaced by co-CEOs David Zinsner and Michelle Johnston Holthaus.

Gelsinger, 63, took the reins as CEO in 2021 at a company beset with challenges. He embarked on an ambitious turnaround plan for Intel (INTC), trying to restore its lead in making the smallest and fastest chips and establishing a foundry business.

But Gelsinger was reportedly forced out after Intel's (INTC) board of directors lost confidence in his plan to turn the company around.

According to the report, the conflict was heightened last week after Gelsinger met with the board to discuss how to win back market share and narrow the gap between itself and Nvidia (NVDA). He was then given the option to retire or be removed.

The American chipmaking icon announced in August that it was letting go 15% of its staff and cut its spending and headcount by $10 billion as it works to streamline its operations.

In the most recently reported quarter, the company said it lost $0.46 per share as revenue fell 6.2% year-over-year to $13.28 billion.

Intel (INTC) shares are down over 58% YTD. In comparison, the S&P 500 has risen nearly 28%, while Nvidia (NVDA) has nearly tripled.

data.title: Omnicom is said to near mega-merger with Interpublic
data.description:
Omnicom Group (NYSE:OMC) is in advanced talks to buy longtime rival Interpublic Group (NYSE:IPG) in a deal that would create the world’s biggest advertising company, The Wall Street Journal reported Sunday, citing people familiar with the matter.

A transaction could be announced as early as this week, according the newspaper’s sources.

Seeking Alpha has sought comment from Omnicom (OMC) and Interpublic (IPG), and will update this story accordingly.

The all-stock agreement is likely to value Interpublic (IPG) in a range of $13 billion to $14 billion excluding debt, the Journal reported. Interpublic’s (IPG) market value was about $11 billion as of Friday.

A combined company would have net revenue of more than $20 billion a year, based on 2023 figures for each company.

Omnicom (OMC) is the world’s third-largest ad company by revenue, while Interpublic (IPG) is the fourth-biggest. A combination would overtake WPP (NYSE:WPP) as the industry’s biggest player. WPP’s net revenue last year was about $15.1 billion, the Journal reported.

The combined company also would have some of Madison Avenue’s best-known advertising agencies under the same umbrella after years of mergers and acquisitions.

Omnicom’s (OMC) agency brands include BBDO, TBWA, FleishmanHillard and ad buyer Omnicom Media Group. Its marquee clients include Disney, Amazon, AT&T and PepsiCo.

Interpublic (IPG) owns agencies such as McCann Worldgroup, Weber Shandwick, FCB and ad-buying firm Mediabrands. Its clients include L’Oréal, Johnson & Johnson and Geico.

data.title: Microsoft's plan to win the console war: Make everything an Xbox
data.description:
"This is an Xbox" declares Microsoft's (NASDAQ:MSFT) latest ad campaign as it showcases a variety of devices running Xbox games, its latest effort to declare that every screen is now an Xbox.

The tech giant has been trying to expand Xbox to multiple devices for over five years now, as it positions the Xbox brand as a platform to play games anywhere instead of just an Xbox console.

"Whether you have an Xbox console, play on PC, with Samsung Smart TVs, handhelds, mobile phones, Amazon Fire TV, or a Meta Quest headset with Game Pass Ultimate, you can play with Xbox," the company said in a statement.

"Should Microsoft expand its partnerships to include Chromecast, Roku, Apple TV, and other television operating systems, it should expand its addressable market to over 1 billion households from fewer than 300 million now," said Wedbush analyst Michael Pachter.

"I think this is highly likely to occur, and expect Microsoft to expand its subscriber base to 200 million within 10 years," said Pachter.

Can Xbox Catch Up?

Microsoft's (MSFT) latest Xbox Series S and X consoles still lag Sony (SONY) and Nintendo (OTCPK:NTDOF) in sales numbers.

The company's much hyped Xbox Game Pass Subscription service has a target of 100 million subscribers by 2030, but it last announced numbers in 2022 when the service hit 25 million users.

According to an IGN report, Game Pass had 33 million subscribers at the end of 2023, representing growth of 13%, down from 15% in 2022.

Mobile Dreams

A big part of Microsoft's (MSFT) "Xbox everywhere" strategy is a mobile Xbox store. The company has been hinting at a "next-generation" for years, with its acquisition of $68.7 billion acquisition of Activision Blizzard only adding fuel to the fire.

Activision Blizzard library consists of mobile heavy hitters like Candy Crush and Call of Duty, which could help the company draw away users from Apple (AAPL) App Store and Google's (GOOG) (GOOGL) Play Store.

But the company's efforts seem to have been stymied by the dominant app stores' policies, on pause until regulatory cases involving the two stores work their way through courts in various countries.

No More Exclusives?

Microsoft (MSFT) has also released some of its games on Sony's (SONY) PlayStation and Nintendo's (OTCPK:NTDOF) Switch consoles, a rather controversial move for diehard Xbox fans.

Console makers often release games exclusive to their platforms to entice consumers to choose their devices.

In an interview with Bloomberg, Xbox chief and self-declared gamer Phil Spencer said that he was pleased with the results. "I do not see sort of red lines in our portfolio that say 'thou must not,'" he said.

"To reach new players, we need to be creative and adaptive of new business models, new devices, new ways of access. We’re not going to grow the market with $1,000 consoles," Spencer said.

More on Microsoft

data.title: CVC Capital Partners considering takeover of CompuGroup Medical: report
Ant Group finance chief Cyril Han to take over as CEO in March
data.description:
Ant Group said Sunday that President and Chief Financial Officer Cyril Han will take over as the chief executive early next year.

China’s largest mobile payments system operator said in a statement that Han will take over from Eric Jing on March 1.

Jing will remain chair of the company.

Han joined Ant’s Alipay unit in 2014.

Ant is an affiliate of Alibaba Group Holding (BABA).
